articles.description: Industry leaders backed PM Modi's appeal for fuel conservation amid rising energy prices. They stressed no need for panic over fuel availability, praised the government for stable prices, and urged people to avoid wasteful consumption.

articles.title: Gurugram Sustains Real Estate Growth Amid Rapid Urban Expansion
articles.description: Delhi NCR [India], May 12: Few cities in India have transformed as rapidly as Gurugram. Over the last two decades, the city has evolved from a peripheral satellite town into one of the country's most influential corporate and residential hubs, attracting multinational companies, institutional capital and a constantly expanding urban population.

articles.title: Explained: Why Vedanta shares are up 4% after government's royalty cut on crude oil
articles.description: Vedanta shares rallied after the Centre reduced royalty rates on crude oil and natural gas production, a move expected to lower costs for the company’s Rajasthan fields and support upstream exploration. Brokerage CLSA said the royalty cut could significantly benefit Vedanta, while investors also await the listing of its demerged entities.

articles.title: How to rebalance my portfolio in current environment?
articles.description: ET Wealth Reader's Query: How to rebalance portfolios in the current environment—between equity, debt, gold, and global assets—to manage both risk and return?

articles.title: Can Reliance Jio's fresh-issue play restore faith in India's billion-dollar IPOs?
articles.description: Reliance Jio may change its IPO plan. Instead of existing investors selling shares, it could be a fresh issue. This means money goes directly into Jio Platforms for debt reduction and expansion. This move could restore investor faith in large Indian IPOs. It might set a new standard for future mega listings. The market will watch this development closely.

articles.title: Homestays in and around Hampi inspected
articles.description: Vijayanagara SP S Jahnavi leads a major inspection of Hampi homestays. Out of 133 units, 132 are operating without mandatory Tourism Department registration.

articles.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.55 as fraying US-Iran ceasefire keeps oil prices on the boil
articles.description: The rupee has reached its lowest point ever against the US dollar. This decline is driven by investor concerns over escalating conflict in the Middle East. The situation has led to a surge in oil prices. This development poses economic challenges for India, which imports most of its energy needs. The rupee's new low reflects these growing worries.

articles.title: Delhi HC sets up task force to make revamp plan for Anand Vihar bus terminal
articles.description: The court instructed the PWD to engage a qualified architect to prepare a detailed redevelopment plan, including sketches and 3D electronic representations of the proposed landscaping.

articles.title: Carl Bildt: India's growth driving deeper engagement with Europe
articles.description: Former Swedish PM Carl Bildt said India's growth is driving deeper ties with Europe, with Swedish firms planning to expand post-FTA. He called India indispensable for the global economy and urged it to use its diplomacy in the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: Commentary: Japan’s cheap curry lunch faces an impossible trinity
articles.description: Japan can preserve only two of three things: cheap everyday services, rising wages and minimal immigration, says Gearoid Reidy for Bloomberg Opinion.

articles.title: Rs 76,000 Crore Annual Profit Of State Oil Firms Could Be Wiped Out By Q1 Losses
articles.description: A report said losses suffered by IOC, BPCL and HPCL in April-June quarter may wipe out their entire annual profit of Rs 76,000 crore.

articles.title: Stocks edge up ahead of US-China meeting; oil rallies on US-Iran stalemate
articles.description: On Wall Street, the S&P 500 rose 13.91 points, or 0.19%, to 7,412.84 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 27.05 points, or 0.10%, to 26,274.13, record closing highs for both.

articles.title: Hedge funds record decade-high weekly buying in Korea, Japan, Taiwan equities, Morgan Stanley says
articles.description: Global hedge funds significantly increased their investments in South Korea, Japan, and Taiwan last week, reaching a decade-high in stock buying. This surge, driven by AI beneficiaries and the region's dominance in the tech supply chain, saw net exposure to these markets hit its highest point since 2010.

articles.title: Consumer appliance manufacturer Mekr Technologies raises Rs 67 crore from Avaana Capital, Titan
articles.description: Mekr Technologies, a consumer appliance startup, has secured Rs 67 crore in funding. This capital will boost manufacturing capacity and expand exports of Indian-made home and kitchen appliances. The company aims to reduce import dependency by indigenizing product manufacturing. Mekr partners with major brands, handling design, engineering, and assembly. This investment supports R&D, supplier localization, and automation for global markets.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari Govt's 1st Cabinet Meet: Land for BSF, Ayushman Bharat
articles.description: In its first meeting, West Bengal's new cabinet led by CM Suvendu Adhikari approved land transfer to BSF for border fencing and the state's implementation of the Ayushman Bharat scheme. Six key decisions were made in the first meeting.

articles.title: Relief for East Delhi commuters: New 13-km Delhi Metro corridor to connect THESE stations | Check details
articles.description: A 13 kilometer long corridor has been approved by the Delhi government. With its eight stations, three interchange points and a mix of underground and elevated sections, the corridor is expected to ease commuters for residents across densely populated East Delhi neighbourhoods.

articles.title: JMM takes a dig on PM’s appeal on cutting fuel use, other curbs
articles.description: The Jharkhand Mukti Morcha (JMM) has thrown shade on Prime Minister Modi's recent plea for fuel conservation, pointing out the contradiction posed by the BJP's elaborate campaign events in Gujarat. The party accused the central government of deceiving the public with unfulfilled promises, thereby fueling economic challenges, skyrocketing prices, and job scarcity.

articles.title: Domestic violence Act doesn’t confer ownership rights over property: HC
articles.description: Delhi HC ruled the DV Act’s “right of residence” doesn’t grant property ownership; upheld eviction of daughter-in-law, balancing senior citizens’ rights and alternate housing.

articles.title: Click, Pay, Stab: Delhi’s Online Button Knife Boom
articles.description: Delhi sees rise in stabbings linked to button knives bought online for as low as Rs 87; police crack down as e-commerce listings evade Arms Act rules.

articles.title: Neglected injury costs mobility, surgeon reconstructs wrist using patient’s bone
articles.description: Delhi surgeons at Max Hospital reconstructed a neglected scaphoid fracture using a proximal hamate bone graft, restoring wrist stability and mobility in a rare procedure.

articles.title: Court Orders Police to Submit Footage Cited Before High Court in Tarun Butolia Case
articles.description: Delhi court orders police to submit video footage cited before Delhi High Court in Tarun Butolia Uttam Nagar case after threats, caste abuse claims.
